title: enlista {Redactum}
text: last word begin “”. last word begins “”. last word begins “”. last word starts character letter. vector consists numeric values.
code: x <- c("Pedro", "Juan", "Mario", "Emilio", "Iker", "Hugo") xx <- Redactum::enlista(x) xx #> [1] "Pedro, Juan, Mario, Emilio, Iker y Hugo"  # Example: combine it with paste0(). xx <- paste0("Los amigos de Armando son ", xx,".") xx #> [1] "Los amigos de Armando son Pedro, Juan, Mario, Emilio, Iker y Hugo." x <- c("Pedro", "Juan", "Mario", "Emilio", "Iker") xx <- Redactum::enlista(x) xx #> [1] "Pedro, Juan, Mario, Emilio e Iker"  # Example: combine it with paste0(). xx <- paste0("Los amigos de Armando son ", xx,".") xx #> [1] "Los amigos de Armando son Pedro, Juan, Mario, Emilio e Iker." x <- c("península", "cordillera", "isla") xx <- Redactum::enlista(x) xx #> [1] "península, cordillera e isla"  # Example: combine it with paste0(). xx <- paste0("Los accidentes geográficos encontrados son: ", xx,".") xx #> [1] "Los accidentes geográficos encontrados son: península, cordillera e isla." x <- c("península", "cordillera", "123") xx <- Redactum::enlista(x) xx #> [1] "península, cordillera y 123"  # Example: combine it with paste0(). xx <- paste0("Los accidentes geográficos encontrados son: ", xx,".") xx #> [1] "Los accidentes geográficos encontrados son: península, cordillera y 123." x <- c(345, 678, 123, 0) xx <- Redactum::enlista(x) xx #> [1] "345, 678, 123 y 0"  # Example: combine it with paste0(). xx <- paste0("Los accidentes geográficos estan numerados como: ", xx,".") xx #> [1] "Los accidentes geográficos estan numerados como: 345, 678, 123 y 0."

title: ncient {Redactum}
text: Abbreviation used scientific names. Unspecified species within genus, using abbreviation “sp.” “sp” without period. Unspecified species within genus, using abbreviation “spp.” “spp” without period.
code: ncient("Homo sapiens") #> [1] "H. sapiens"  name <- "Hydrolagus melanophasma" ncient(name) #> [1] "H. melanophasma" # With a period ncient("Hydrolagus sp.") #> [1] "Hydrolagus sp."  # Without a period ncient("Hydrolagus sp") #> [1] "Hydrolagus sp" # With a period ncient("Hydrolagus spp.") #> [1] "Hydrolagus spp."  # Without a period ncient("Hydrolagus spp") #> [1] "Hydrolagus spp"

title: nnum {Redactum}
text: Return name associated number 2 English. Return name associated number 0 English. Return name associated number 2 Spanish. Return name associated number 0 Spanish.
code: nnames <- c("one", "two", "three",             "four", "five", "six",             "seven", "eight", "nine",             "ten", "zero")  x <- 2  nnum(x,nnames) #> [1] "two" nnames <- c("one", "two", "three",             "four", "five", "six",             "seven", "eight", "nine",             "ten", "zero")  x <- 0  nnum(x,nnames) #> [1] "zero" nnames <- c("uno", "dos", "tres",             "cuatro", "cinco", "seis",             "siete", "ocho", "nueve",             "diez","cero")  x <- 2  nnum(x,nnames) #> [1] "dos" nnames <- c("uno", "dos", "tres",             "cuatro", "cinco", "seis",             "siete", "ocho", "nueve",             "diez","cero")  x <- 0  nnum(x,nnames) #> [1] "cero"

title: Tools to simplify writing compelling manuscripts in Spanish and in other languages
text: Abbreviated scientific name abbreviated name derived scientific name. create abbreviation, first letter genus taken, written uppercase, followed period. specific name added space scientific name. Within function, condition established. scientific name refers species specified particular genus, Hydrolagus sp., multiple species within genus, like Hydrolagus spp., name abbreviated. However, scientific name designates specific species, modified; instance, Hydrolagus melanophasma abbreviated H. melanophasma. function operates correctly without periods abbreviations, “sp” “sp.”

title: enlista — enlista
text: function included Redactum package.

title: enlista — enlista
text: x vector contains multiple elements.

title: enlista — enlista
text: vector inserts comma element, adds  'y' last element, last element word  starting '', instead adds 'e'.

title: enlista — enlista
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # The last word does not begin with "I".  x <- c("Pedro", "Juan", "Mario", "Emilio", "Iker", "Hugo")  xx <- enlista(x)   # Combine it with paste0().  paste0("Los amigos de Armando son ", xx,".")   # The last word begins  with "I".  x <- c("Pedro", "Juan", "Mario", "Emilio", "Iker")  xx <- enlista(x)   # The last word begins  with "i".  x <- c("península", "cordillera", "isla")  xx <- enlista(x)   # The last word starts with a character that is not a letter.  x <- c("península", "cordillera", "123")  xx <- enlista(x)   # The vector consists of numeric values.    x <- c(345, 678, 123, 0)    xx <- enlista(x)  } # }

title: ncient — ncient
text: Abbreviated scientific name

title: ncient — ncient
text: name character string containing scientific name.

title: ncient — ncient
text: character string containing abbreviated scientific name.

title: ncient — ncient
text: abbreviated name derived scientific name. create abbreviation, first letter genus taken, written uppercase, followed period. specific name added space scientific name. Within function, condition established. scientific name refers species specified particular genus, Hydrolagus sp., multiple species within genus, like Hydrolagus spp., name abbreviated. However, scientific name designates specific species, modified; instance, Hydrolagus melanophasma abbreviated H. melanophasma. function operates correctly without periods abbreviations, "sp" "sp."

title: ncient — ncient
code: ncient("Homo sapiens") #> [1] "H. sapiens"  name <- "Hydrolagus melanophasma" ncient(name) #> [1] "H. melanophasma"  ## Example: Using the abbreviation "sp" without a period. name <- "Hydrolagus sp" ncient(name) #> [1] "Hydrolagus sp"  ## Example: Using the abbreviation "spp." with a period. name <- "Hydrolagus spp." ncient(name) #> [1] "Hydrolagus spp."

title: nnum — nnum
text: function returns name given number, specifically numbers ranging 0 10. uses array defines names numbers one ten, including zero end. user specifies names numbers chosen language.

title: nnum — nnum
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Number in English. nnames <- c("one", "two", "three",  "four", "five", "six",   "seven", "eight", "nine",    "ten", "zero")  x <- 2 nnum(x,nnames)  ## Number in Spanish. nnames <- c("uno", "dos", "tres",  "cuatro", "cinco", "seis",  "siete", "ocho", "nueve",   "diez","cero")  x <- 2 nnum(x,nnames)   } # }
